resources.string: Please give your favorite a name
A favorite with this name already exists
Are you sure you want to remove this favorite?
Image is less than 5 minutes old. Still refresh?
Set the default view on application startup.
Select the favorite radar to show.
When to automatically refresh radar images. Enabling this will use more data.
Prompt to refresh when manually refreshing images.
Tap the edge of the image to view cut-off portions.
Hides the top bar. Drag from the left side of the screen to access the sidebar or hold to access the context menu.
Apply dark styling to the overlay when in dark mode
Weather Radar is provided free of charge and with no advertisements. If you find it useful, please consider supporting my work.

Weather Radar is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

Weather Radar is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
Weather Radar provides access to doppler radar images from the United States National Weather Service. Their privacy policy can be found here:\nwww.weather.gov/privacy
This application is not endorsed by the National Weather Service. Their images are public domain and not subject to copyright protection:\nwww.weather.gov/disclaimer
National Weather Service radar imagery is only available for the United States and its territories.

Weather Radar provides access to the air quality map at fire.airnow.gov. AirNow is a collaboration of various government agencies and this application is not endorsed by or affiliated with that partnership or any of the participating agencies. You can view their disclaimer here:\nwww.airnow.gov/fasm-v4/disclaimer
Or for more information about AirNow, visit:\nwww.airnow.gov/about-airnow
Weather Radar provides access to doppler radar images from the United States National Weather Service.\n
